_note: EVADE INPUT-CONTROL TEST (offline-mapped hook 0x30F49C, now built into the harness). Goal: prove the engine's NATIVE avoidance roll honors evade bytes we write on the target just before the VM reads them. Method: at 0x30F49C (last real instr before the VM roll 0x30FA34; rbx=target) force Ramza's (charId 0x01) evade INPUT bytes to class-evade=100, all other sources 0 (+0x46=0 +0x47=0 +0x4A=0 +0x4B=100 +0x4E=0). NO output-control hooks are used (pre-clamp OFF, selector control OFF) - only the input write. The selector PROBE is on (observe-only) to log the RESULTING evadeType. EvadeInputProbe defaults (Rva 0x30F49C, ExpectedBytes '48 8B D3 88 4B 41') come from code, so they are omitted here. RUN: relaunch the game, then make Agrias->Ramza (front-on, a normally-guaranteed hit) the FIRST attack that targets Ramza. PASS = Ramza plays a class-evade 'Miss' and takes 0 damage NATIVELY (selector log evadeType=0x04, evade-input log before 4B=00 -> after 4B=64). FAIL = Ramza takes the hit (selector log evadeType=0x00, damage applies) => the VM used a cached copy, input-control of evade is not viable and we fall back to data-disable + output-control. Safe: one guarded write on one target; requires a fresh launch (asm bakes immediates at mod load).
